af.name: Afghanistan
af.notes: Type C (Europlug) is most common. Type F (Schuko) in newer construction.

al.name: Albania

plugTypes.note: Compatible with Type F, common for low-power devices

al.notes: Type F (Schuko) is the primary standard. Type C plugs work in Type F sockets.

dz.name: Algeria
dz.notes: Type F (Schuko) is primary. French colonial legacy mixed with modern German standard.

ar.name: Argentina

plugTypes.note: Standard in all modern installations since 1998

ar.notes: Argentina switched to Type I (three angled flat pins) in 1998. Type C exists in very old buildings but don't rely on it. Type I is mandatory for all new construction.

am.name: Armenia
am.notes: Type F dominates following European standardization.

au.name: Australia
au.notes: Type I exclusively. Mandatory safety shutters in all outlets.

at.name: Austria
at.notes: Type F (Schuko) dominates. Type C compatible for low-power devices.

az.name: Azerbaijan
az.notes: Type F is standard following post-Soviet modernization.

bs.name: Bahamas

plugTypes.note: Modern standard, required in new construction
Still common, works in Type B sockets

bs.notes: Type B dominates in modern buildings. Type A plugs work in Type B sockets.

bh.name: Bahrain
bh.notes: Type G exclusively, following British colonial legacy.

bd.name: Bangladesh
bd.notes: Type G is primary due to British colonial influence.

bb.name: Barbados
bb.notes: Type B is primary. Unusual 50Hz frequency with low voltage.

by.name: Belarus
by.notes: Type F dominates following European standardization.

be.name: Belgium
be.notes: Type E with grounding pin in socket. Type C compatible.

bz.name: Belize
bz.notes: US standard. Some areas have 220V for heavy appliances.

bj.name: Benin
bj.notes: Type E dominates due to French colonial influence.

bt.name: Bhutan
bt.notes: Type D dominant, following Indian influence.

bo.name: Bolivia
bo.notes: Mixed voltage system. Type A most common despite higher voltage.

ba.name: Bosnia and Herzegovina
ba.notes: Type F dominates following European integration.

bw.name: Botswana

plugTypes.note: South African influence for heavy appliances

bw.notes: Type D most common, transitioning to Type G in newer areas.

br.name: Brazil

plugTypes.note: Official standard since 2011, mandatory in all new construction

br.notes: Brazil adopted Type N (NBR 14136) in 2011. All new buildings use Type N. Voltage varies by region (127V south, 220V north).

bn.name: Brunei
bn.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial standard maintained.

bg.name: Bulgaria
bg.notes: Type F dominates following EU standardization.

bf.name: Burkina Faso
bf.notes: Type E dominates due to French colonial influence.

bi.name: Burundi
bi.notes: Type C most widespread, some Type E from colonial period.

cv.name: Cabo Verde
cv.notes: Type F is standard, following European influence.

kh.name: Cambodia
kh.notes: Type A dominates despite higher voltage. Mixed standards reflect various influences.

cm.name: Cameroon
cm.notes: Type E dominates due to French colonial influence.

ca.name: Canada

plugTypes.note: Required in all new construction since 1965
Legacy, works in Type B sockets

ca.notes: Type B is standard for safety/grounding. Type A plugs work in Type B sockets.

cf.name: Central African Republic
cf.notes: Type E dominates due to French colonial influence.

td.name: Chad
td.notes: Type E dominates due to French colonial influence.

cl.name: Chile
cl.notes: Type C dominates. Type L found in some areas due to Italian influence.

cn.name: China

plugTypes.note: Most common, ubiquitous in hotels and public spaces

cn.notes: Type A is most prevalent, especially in hotels and tourist areas. Type I for grounded devices. Type C exists but much less common.

co.name: Colombia
co.notes: Type A dominates. Type B increasing in new construction for safety.

km.name: Comoros
km.notes: Type E standard following French influence.

cg.name: Congo
cg.notes: Type E dominates due to French colonial influence.

cd.name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
cd.notes: Type C most widespread, mixed with Type E in some regions.

ck.name: Cook Islands
ck.notes: Type I exclusively, following New Zealand administration.

cr.name: Costa Rica
cr.notes: Type B is primary for safety. Type A plugs work in Type B sockets.

ci.name: Côte d'Ivoire
ci.notes: Type E dominates due to French colonial influence.

hr.name: Croatia
hr.notes: Type F dominates following EU standardization.

cu.name: Cuba
cu.notes: Type A dominates but Type B increasing. Mixed voltage system.

cy.name: Cyprus
cy.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial legacy maintained.

cz.name: Czech Republic
cz.notes: Type F dominates following EU integration.

dk.name: Denmark

plugTypes.note: Danish standard with side grounding pin

dk.notes: Type K is uniquely Danish. Type C plugs work in Type K sockets.

dj.name: Djibouti
dj.notes: Type E standard following French influence.

dm.name: Dominica
dm.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial legacy.

do.name: Dominican Republic
do.notes: Type B is primary. US electrical standard.

ec.name: Ecuador
ec.notes: Type A dominates. Type B growing in new construction.

eg.name: Egypt
eg.notes: Type C dominates. Type F in modern tourist infrastructure.

sv.name: El Salvador
sv.notes: Type B is primary following US standard.

gq.name: Equatorial Guinea
gq.notes: Type E standard due to Spanish/French colonial influence.

er.name: Eritrea
er.notes: Type C dominates with some Italian Type L influence.

ee.name: Estonia
ee.notes: Type F dominates following EU integration.

sz.name: Eswatini
sz.notes: Type M dominates due to South African influence.

et.name: Ethiopia
et.notes: Type C is most common standard.

fj.name: Fiji
fj.notes: Type I exclusively, following Australian influence.

fi.name: Finland
fi.notes: Type F dominates. Type C compatible for low-power devices.

fr.name: France

plugTypes.note: Compatible with Type E, common in older buildings

fr.notes: Type E with grounding pin in socket vs. Type F's side clips. Type C compatible.

gm.name: Gambia
gm.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial legacy.

ge.name: Georgia
ge.notes: Type F dominates following European integration.

de.name: Germany
de.notes: Type F (Schuko) dominates. Type C compatible for low-power devices.

gh.name: Ghana
gh.notes: Type G dominates due to British colonial influence.

gr.name: Greece
gr.notes: Type F dominates following European standardization.

gd.name: Grenada
gd.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial legacy.

gt.name: Guatemala
gt.notes: Type B is primary following US standard.

gn.name: Guinea
gn.notes: Type E dominates due to French colonial influence.

gw.name: Guinea-Bissau
gw.notes: Type C is the primary standard.

gy.name: Guyana
gy.notes: Type G primary, but mixed voltage system with some US influence.

ht.name: Haiti
ht.notes: Type A dominates. Mixed voltage system.

hn.name: Honduras
hn.notes: Type B is primary following US standard.

hk.name: Hong Kong
hk.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial legacy maintained.

hu.name: Hungary
hu.notes: Type F dominates following EU integration.

is.name: Iceland
is.notes: Type F dominates. Type C compatible.

in.name: India

plugTypes.note: Most common, especially in homes and hotels
Some modern buildings, varies by region

in.notes: Type D (three round pins, 5mm) is most common for general use. Type M (larger version) for high-power appliances. Type C found in some modern buildings but not universal.

id.name: Indonesia
id.notes: Type C dominates. Type F in modern tourist infrastructure.

ir.name: Iran
ir.notes: Type C is the primary standard.

iq.name: Iraq
iq.notes: Type D primary due to British influence. Type G in newer areas.

ie.name: Ireland
ie.notes: Type G exclusively, following UK standard.

im.name: Isle of Man
im.notes: Type G exclusively, British Crown dependency.

il.name: Israel
il.notes: Israel uses exclusively Type H (three flat pins in triangle). Very few exceptions. Type C adapters won't work - need specific Type H adapter.

it.name: Italy

plugTypes.note: Schuko standard, most common in modern buildings
Italian standard, especially for high-power devices
Works in most Italian outlets but not always secure fit

it.notes: Italy primarily uses Type F (Schuko) and Type L (Italian 3-pin). Type C Europlugs work in most outlets but may not fit securely. Type L has 10A and 16A variants.

jm.name: Jamaica
jm.notes: Type B is primary. Unusual 50Hz frequency with US voltage.

jp.name: Japan

plugTypes.note: Standard for most appliances and outlets
Grounded outlets, mainly for heavy appliances

jp.notes: Japan primarily uses Type A. Type B exists mainly for grounded appliances but most outlets are Type A only. Unique 100V voltage requires converters for many devices.

je.name: Jersey
je.notes: Type G exclusively, British Crown dependency.

jo.name: Jordan
jo.notes: Type G dominates due to British influence.

kz.name: Kazakhstan
kz.notes: Type F dominates following European integration.

ke.name: Kenya
ke.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial legacy strongly maintained.

kp.name: North Korea
kp.notes: Type C primary. Limited electrical infrastructure.

kr.name: South Korea
kr.notes: Type F is official standard for safety. Type C compatible for small appliances.

kw.name: Kuwait
kw.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial influence.

kg.name: Kyrgyzstan
kg.notes: Type C primary with some Type F modernization.

la.name: Laos
la.notes: Type A dominates despite higher voltage. Mixed standards.

lv.name: Latvia
lv.notes: Type F dominates following EU integration.

lb.name: Lebanon
lb.notes: Type D primary, Type G growing in modern areas.

ls.name: Lesotho
ls.notes: Type M exclusively, following South African standard.

lr.name: Liberia
lr.notes: Type B primary due to US historical influence.

ly.name: Libya
ly.notes: Type D primary. Complex voltage system varies by region.

lt.name: Lithuania
lt.notes: Type F dominates following EU integration.

lu.name: Luxembourg
lu.notes: Type F dominates. Type C compatible.

mo.name: Macao
mo.notes: Type G primary, British colonial influence maintained despite Chinese administration.

mg.name: Madagascar
mg.notes: Type C dominates with some French Type E influence.

mw.name: Malawi
mw.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial legacy.

my.name: Malaysia
my.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial standard maintained.

mv.name: Maldives
mv.notes: Type D primary, Type G in tourist areas.

ml.name: Mali
ml.notes: Type E dominates due to French colonial influence.

mt.name: Malta
mt.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial legacy maintained.

mh.name: Marshall Islands
mh.notes: Type B primary following US administration.

mr.name: Mauritania
mr.notes: Type C is the primary standard.

mu.name: Mauritius
mu.notes: Type G dominates, British colonial legacy.

mx.name: Mexico
mx.notes: Type B is primary in modern buildings. Type A still common in residential areas.

fm.name: Micronesia
fm.notes: Type B primary following US administration.

md.name: Moldova
md.notes: Type F dominates following European integration.

mn.name: Mongolia
mn.notes: Type C primary with some Type E variation.

me.name: Montenegro
me.notes: Type F dominates following European integration.

ma.name: Morocco
ma.notes: Type E is official standard due to French colonial influence. Type C compatible.

mz.name: Mozambique
mz.notes: Type C primary with mixed South African and European influence.

mm.name: Myanmar
mm.notes: Type D primary due to British colonial influence.

na.name: Namibia
na.notes: Type M exclusively, following South African standard.

np.name: Nepal
np.notes: Type D dominates following Indian influence.

nl.name: Netherlands
nl.notes: Type F (Schuko) dominates. Type C compatible for low-power devices.

nz.name: New Zealand
nz.notes: Type I exclusively. Mandatory safety shutters in all outlets.

ni.name: Nicaragua
ni.notes: Type B is primary following US standard.

ne.name: Niger
ne.notes: Type E dominates due to French colonial influence.

ng.name: Nigeria
ng.notes: Type G is official standard due to British colonial influence.

nu.name: Niue
nu.notes: Type I following New Zealand administration.

mk.name: North Macedonia
mk.notes: Type F dominates following European integration.

mp.name: Northern Mariana Islands
mp.notes: Type B primary following US administration.

no.name: Norway
no.notes: Type F dominates. Type C compatible for low-power devices.

pk.name: Pakistan
pk.notes: Type D primary due to British colonial influence. Type G growing in urban areas.

pw.name: Palau
pw.notes: Type B primary following US administration.

ps.name: Palestine
ps.notes: Type H dominates due to Israeli electrical infrastructure.

pa.name: Panama
pa.notes: Type B is primary following US standard.

pg.name: Papua New Guinea
pg.notes: Type I exclusively, Australian colonial legacy.

pe.name: Peru
pe.notes: Type A dominates despite higher voltage. Some Type B in modern construction.

ph.name: Philippines
ph.notes: Type A dominates despite higher voltage. Type B increasing in modern buildings.

pn.name: Pitcairn Islands
pn.notes: Type I following New Zealand administration.

pl.name: Poland
pl.notes: Type F dominates following EU integration.

pt.name: Portugal

plugTypes.note: Legacy but still common, works in Type F

pt.notes: Type F dominates in modern construction. Type C legacy but compatible.

pr.name: Puerto Rico
pr.notes: Type B primary following US standard.

ro.name: Romania
ro.notes: Type F dominates following EU integration.

ru.name: Russia
ru.notes: Type F dominates following European standardization.

rw.name: Rwanda
rw.notes: Type C primary with some Type J variation.

sh.name: Saint Helena
sh.notes: Type G exclusively, British overseas territory.

kn.name: Saint Kitts and Nevis
kn.notes: Type G primary, British colonial legacy.

lc.name: Saint Lucia
lc.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial legacy.

pm.name: Saint Pierre and Miquelon
pm.notes: Type E exclusively, French overseas territory.

vc.name: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
vc.notes: Type G primary, mixed voltage system.

ws.name: Samoa
ws.notes: Type I exclusively, New Zealand/Australian influence.

sm.name: San Marino
sm.notes: Type F primary, following Italian and European standards.

st.name: São Tomé and Príncipe
st.notes: Type C primary with some Type F modernization.

sa.name: Saudi Arabia
sa.notes: Type G exclusively, British technical influence.

sn.name: Senegal
sn.notes: Type E dominates due to French colonial influence.

rs.name: Serbia
rs.notes: Type F dominates following European integration.

sc.name: Seychelles
sc.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial legacy.

sl.name: Sierra Leone
sl.notes: Type G dominates due to British colonial influence.

sg.name: Singapore
sg.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial standard maintained.

sx.name: Sint Maarten
sx.notes: Type A primary in Dutch Caribbean territories.

sk.name: Slovakia
sk.notes: Type F dominates following EU integration.

si.name: Slovenia
si.notes: Type F dominates following EU integration.

sb.name: Solomon Islands
sb.notes: Type G primary due to British colonial influence.

so.name: Somalia
so.notes: Type C primary. Limited electrical infrastructure.

za.name: South Africa
za.notes: Type M is the official standard and dominates. Type C in universal sockets for tourists.

gs.name: South Georgia
gs.notes: Type G exclusively, British overseas territory.

ss.name: South Sudan
ss.notes: Type C primary. Limited electrical infrastructure.

es.name: Spain

plugTypes.note: Legacy but still common, works in Type F

es.notes: Type F dominates in modern construction. Type C legacy but compatible.

lk.name: Sri Lanka
lk.notes: Type G dominates due to British colonial influence.

sd.name: Sudan
sd.notes: Type C primary with some British Type D influence.

sr.name: Suriname
sr.notes: Type C primary despite low voltage. Dutch colonial influence.

se.name: Sweden
se.notes: Type F dominates. Type C compatible for low-power devices.

ch.name: Switzerland

plugTypes.note: Works in Type J sockets for low power

ch.notes: Type J is uniquely Swiss standard. Type C plugs work in Type J sockets.

sy.name: Syria
sy.notes: Type C primary with some French Type E influence.

tw.name: Taiwan
tw.notes: Type A dominates. Type B for grounded appliances in modern buildings.

tj.name: Tajikistan
tj.notes: Type C primary with gradual Type F modernization.

tz.name: Tanzania
tz.notes: Type G dominates due to British colonial influence.

th.name: Thailand

plugTypes.note: Most common, especially in hotels and tourist areas
Thai-specific standard, less common in practice

th.notes: Despite Type O being the 'official' Thai standard, Types A and B (American-style) are actually dominant in practice, especially in hotels, malls, and tourist areas. Type C also common.

tl.name: Timor-Leste
tl.notes: Type C primary due to Portuguese colonial influence.

tg.name: Togo
tg.notes: Type C is the primary standard.

tk.name: Tokelau
tk.notes: Type I following New Zealand administration.

to.name: Tonga
to.notes: Type I following Australian/New Zealand influence.

tt.name: Trinidad and Tobago
tt.notes: Type B is primary following US standard.

tn.name: Tunisia
tn.notes: Type E dominates due to French colonial influence.

tr.name: Turkey
tr.notes: Type F (Schuko) is primary standard. Type C compatible and common.

tm.name: Turkmenistan
tm.notes: Type B primary despite higher voltage. Mixed US and European influence.

tc.name: Turks and Caicos Islands
tc.notes: Type B primary following US standard.

ug.name: Uganda
ug.notes: Type G exclusively, British colonial legacy.

ua.name: Ukraine
ua.notes: Type F dominates following European integration.

ae.name: United Arab Emirates
ae.notes: Type G exclusively, British technical influence.

gb.name: United Kingdom
gb.notes: Type G exclusively. Most secure plug system globally, mandatory shutters.

us.name: United States

plugTypes.note: Required in all new construction since 1965
Legacy in older buildings, still functional

us.notes: Type B required in new construction for safety. Type A plugs work in Type B sockets.

uy.name: Uruguay
uy.notes: Type C dominates. Some Type F in modern construction.

uz.name: Uzbekistan
uz.notes: Type C primary with gradual Type F modernization.

va.name: Vatican City
va.notes: Type F primary, following Italian and European standards.

ve.name: Venezuela
ve.notes: Type A dominates. Type B increasing in new construction.

vn.name: Vietnam
vn.notes: Type A dominates despite higher voltage. Type C common in tourist areas. Mixed French colonial legacy.

vi.name: US Virgin Islands
vi.notes: Type B primary following US standard.

eh.name: Western Sahara
eh.notes: Type C primary. Limited electrical infrastructure.

ye.name: Yemen
ye.notes: Type D primary due to British influence. Limited electrical infrastructure.

zm.name: Zambia
zm.notes: Type C primary with mixed British colonial influence.

zw.name: Zimbabwe
zw.notes: Type G dominates due to British colonial influence.
